mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering short-term equity sell-offs as investors price in recession risks within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC below -0.03, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during low-inflation expansions signals robust capacity utilization without margin compression, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of low-inflation expansion periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures directly boost corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as earnings models are revised upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods with rising RSAFS, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, suppressing investment and consumption and driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high GDP growth indicates supply bottlenecks and inventory depletion, triggering short-term equity rallies as firms raise prices and margins expand.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during high GDP growth periods yield negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the regime-specific predictive edge disappears in walk-forward validation.
